user_message: What's at risk across my portfolio? Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-27 11:49 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery deployment blockers

There's been a change in our timeline for deployment blockers. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-03 11:34 EST
Subject: Re: deployment blockers Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 09:16 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery deployment blockers

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-03-21
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Michelle (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery)

[00:01:30] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into deployment blockers.

[00:08:00] Michelle (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:21:00] Michelle (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:25:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:27:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:32:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:37:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

content.title: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery - deployment blockers

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
